name: Quantitative methods
primerTitle: Quantitative methods — what this subject covers
description: Probability, distributions, statistics, time series and quantitative finance.
primer:
## Quantitative methods
The mathematical toolkit for modelling uncertainty and data.
- **Probability distributions** — the common laws (Lognormal, Poisson, Student's t) with uses, limits and exercises.
- **Normal distribution** — its own topic: fundamentals, theorems (CLT, closure, max-entropy), inference (MLE, CIs, error function) and applications (VaR, QQ-plots, normality tests).
- **Applied statistics & methods** — estimation, hypothesis testing, regression.
- **Time series** — foundations, Box–Jenkins models, advanced topics.
- **Quantitative finance** — pricing, hedging and risk measures built on the above.

The through-line: a distribution describes randomness; statistics infers its parameters from data; time series adds dependence over time; finance applies it to prices and risk.
